task_completion.description: Check if the agent opens or reads the .bash_history file to locate the leaked password.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'cat ~/.bash_history', 'grep', or a text editor open with the contents of the '/home/ga/.bash_history' file.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent selects the correct VeraCrypt volume for modification.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt GUI showing '/home/ga/Volumes/internal_ops.hc' in the volume path field, or a CLI command explicitly targeting this exact path.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent opens the password change interface for the volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt 'Volume Password Change' dialog box visible in the GUI, or a CLI prompt asking for the current and new password for the volume.

task_completion.description: Check if the password change operation completes successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A VeraCrypt GUI popup stating 'Password successfully changed' (or similar success message), or a CLI return prompt indicating successful execution of the password change command without errors.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent edits the .bash_history file to remove the leaked password.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) open with the .bash_history file, showing the deletion of the specific line containing the leak, or a terminal command like 'sed -i' targeting the file.

task_completion.description: Check if the agent saves the sanitized history file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text editor showing a 'saved' or 'wrote lines' message for .bash_history, or a subsequent 'cat .bash_history' command showing the file contents without the leaked password.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual VeraCrypt application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of authentic VeraCrypt GUI windows or valid 'veracrypt' CLI commands being executed, not just a text file or mock script.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modified the actual history file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Commands or editor actions explicitly target '/home/ga/.bash_history' (or '~/.bash_history'), rather than a copy or a different file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent removed only the leaked line, rather than deleting or clearing the entire history file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The .bash_history file still contains other commands; commands like 'rm .bash_history', 'cat /dev/null > .bash_history', or 'history -c' were NOT used as a shortcut.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The volume path is exactly '/home/ga/Volumes/internal_ops.hc'. The history file containing the leak is '/home/ga/.bash_history'. The new target password that must be set is 'SecuredCredential2025!'.
